content.Text: [Sun 2026-04-19 11:11 PDT] Create a new file `dashboard/chaos.html` — a Chaos Testing Simulator for the AgentLens observability dashboard. ## What to build An interactive, self-contained HTML page (uses `styles.css` for theming, same as other dashboard pages) that lets users simulate chaos/failure scenarios against a fleet of AI agents and measure resilience. ## Features 1. **Agent Fleet Setup** — configurable fleet of 5-20 simulated agents with names, types, health bars 2. **Chaos Experiments** — 6 injectable failure types: - 🕐 Latency Spike (adds delay to agent responses) - 💥 Error Burst (random error rate increase) - 🧠 Memory Pressure (simulated OOM conditions) - 🔌 Network Partition (isolates agents from each other) - 📈 Load Surge (10x traffic spike) - ⚡ Cascade Failure (one agent failure triggers neighbors) 3. **Real-time Simulation Canvas** — use HTML5 Canvas to show agents as nodes in a network, with visual indicators for health (green→yellow→red), connections between agents (lines that break during partition), and animated failure propagation 4. **Experiment Controls** — Start/Pause/Reset buttons, speed slider (1x-10x), experiment duration selector 5. **Resilience Metrics** — live-updating panel showing: - Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) - Failure Propagation Rate - Fleet Availability % - Blast Radius (% of agents affected) - Recovery Score (0-100) 6. **Experiment Log** — timestamped event feed showing what happened 7. **Preset Scenarios** — 4 presets: "Gentle Nudge" (minor latency), "Storm" (multi-fault), "Cascading Doom" (chain failures), "Full Chaos" (everything at once) 8. **Resilience Report** — after experiment ends, show a summary card with scores, recommendations, and a grade (A-F) 9. **Proactive Recommendations** — after each experiment, suggest hardening measures based on weaknesses found (e.g., "Add circuit breakers", "Increase timeout buffers", "Deploy redundant agents") ## Technical requirements - Single HTML file, all JS/CSS inline (except linking styles.css) - Use CSS variables from styles.css (--bg-primary, --bg-secondary, --border, --text-primary, --green, --red, --yellow, etc.) - Canvas for the fleet network visualization - No external dependencies - Dark/light theme support via the existing theme system - Navigation header matching other pages — include links to: index.html (🔍 Dashboard), timeline.html (📅 Timeline), command-center.html (🎯 Command Center), errors.html (🐛 Errors), costs.html (💰 Costs), sla.html (📏 SLA), alerts.html (🚨 Alert Rules), runbooks.html (📋 Runbooks), and this page highlighted as current - Responsive design ## Also update `dashboard/index.html` Add a nav link for the chaos simulator. In the header-right div, add this link right after the Runbooks link: ```html 🔥 Chaos ``` ## DO NOT run git commands. Just create/edit the files.
I’m checking the existing dashboard pages and shared styling so the new simulator matches the current structure and theme before I edit anything.
I have the shared header and theme variables. Next I’m checking one of the standalone dashboard pages so the new page follows the same nav, theme toggle, and page-level scripting pattern.
